citations.desc: Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors).
citations.narrative:
During inspection, a tricolored (white + brown + gray), intact female Shih Tzu, with a date of birth of 09-12-2016 and
microchip #933000320457370, was found with thick dark brown deposits of tartar over right and left upper and lower rear
teeth. A thick deposit of tartar was also found on the base of the lower right canine tooth. The licensee stated this dog is
bred and unable to have a dental done until after whelping.
Accumulation of tartar on teeth may lead to discomfort, difficulty chewing, progressive gum disease and tooth loss.
The use of appropriate methods to prevent, control, diagnose and treat tartar accumulation and other dental or disease
issues, and timely correction of those issues when found, is necessary for the overall health and well-being of animals.
Correct by June 15, 2026.

During inspection, the licensee was unable to provide documentation on an annual veterinary exam and findings for an
intact female Shih Tzu with a date of birth of 09-12-2016 and microchip #933000320457370. At the time of inspection, the
licensee stated that this dog had received a veterinary annual exam on 10-10-2025 along with other dogs in the kennel.
The licensee was unable to provide written documentation of an annual exam and findings for this dog conducted on 10-
10-2025 or within the previous 12 months to today’s inspection.
Annual veterinary exams are important to evaluate the health of all breeding stock and to detect diseases or conditions
that may impact the health and well-being of animals.
An annual veterinary exam for all adult dogs within a licensed facility is required and should include a record of exam
findings dated and signed by your veterinarian.
Correct by June 15, 2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the licensee.
n

narrative:
2.40(b)(2) 
   
Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors). 
During inspection, a tricolored (white + brown + gray), intact female Shih Tzu, with a date of birth of 09-12-2016 and 
microchip #933000320457370, was found with thick dark brown deposits of tartar over right and left upper and lower rear 
teeth. A thick deposit of tartar was also found on the base of the lower right canine tooth. The licensee stated this dog is 
bred and unable to have a dental done until after whelping. 
 
Accumulation of tartar on teeth may lead to discomfort, difficulty chewing, progressive gum disease and tooth loss. 
 
The use of appropriate methods to prevent, control, diagnose and treat tartar accumulation and other dental or disease 
issues, and timely correction of those issues when found, is necessary for the overall health and well-being of animals. 
 
Correct by June 15, 2026. 
3.13(a)(2) 
   
Veterinary care for dogs. 
During inspection, the licensee was unable to provide documentation on an annual veterinary exam and findings for an 
intact female Shih Tzu with a date of birth of 09-12-2016 and microchip #933000320457370. At the time of inspection, the 
licensee stated that this dog had received a veterinary annual exam on 10-10-2025 along with other dogs in the kennel. 
The licensee was unable to provide written documentation of an annual exam and findings for this dog conducted on 10-
10-2025 or within the previous 12 months to today’s inspection. 
 
Annual veterinary exams are important to evaluate the health of all breeding stock and to detect diseases or conditions 
that may impact the health and well-being of animals. 
 
An annual veterinary exam for all adult dogs within a licensed facility is required and should include a record of exam 
findings dated and signed by your veterinarian. 
 
Correct by June 15, 2026.

This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the licensee.
